observe_decisions.object_type_reasoning: The SIPaKMeD dataset consists of Pap smear cell images for cervical cancer screening, where the primary structures are individual cells.
observe_decisions.color_mode_reasoning: Since the image is an RGB Pap smear, color can carry diagnostic information about the cellular structures, making per-channel analysis beneficial.
observe_decisions.filtration_reasoning: Pap smear images typically highlight cells that are dark on a lighter background, so sublevel filtration is appropriate to capture these structures.

ph_signals.name: low_persistence_signal
ph_signals.description: Very low average persistence in both H0 and H1 — PH is noisy
ph_signals.reasoning: When average persistence is very low in both dimensions, most PH features are noise (short-lived). Image-based descriptors that bypass PH entirely extract more reliable signal from pixel patterns.

observe_decisions.object_type_reasoning: The image is from the SIPaKMeD dataset, which contains isolated Pap smear cells for cervical cancer screening.
observe_decisions.color_mode_reasoning: The image is in RGB format and color may carry diagnostic information, which can improve accuracy for discrete cell classification.
observe_decisions.filtration_reasoning: Sublevel filtration is suitable because it captures dark-on-light structures, typical for stained cells in cytology images.
